opp.next_step: 75% Confidence 26th May- waiting on product team to confirm "strategic sourcing" release plan- STE Budget review finished by mid june 4th May- drip feed more Agentic Sourcing high level benefits. 16th April Consolidation meeting with Wee Keong, conceptual overviews and road map review- Demo and mutual alignment- WK wants to move as quickly as approval for budget confirmed 3rd Feb- Wee Keong, Anthony Review of future Agentic suite- positive reception and planned budgeting process with Shuck- ROI review 12th Jan Ongoing discussions for Agentic Products as part of 2nd Phase of S2P sale- Agentic Sourcing, Agentic Contracts, others
opp.ais_why:
• The opportunity name itself is 'STE Agentic Suite (phase 2)'. Per the 12 Jan 2026 rep note: 'Ongoing discussions for Agentic Products as part of 2nd Phase of S2P sale- Agentic Sourcing, Agentic Contracts, others.' This is a named-Phase-2 expansion of an existing Zycus customer (ST Engineering) into the Agentic Sourcing and Agentic Contracts modules. The buyer (Wee Keong) is the procurement leader driving this Phase 2.
• On 3 Feb 2026: 'Wee Keong, Anthony Review of future Agentic suite- positive reception and planned budgeting process with Shuck- ROI review.' On 16 Apr 2026: 'Consolidation meeting with Wee Keong, conceptual overviews and road map review- Demo and mutual alignment- WK wants to move as quickly as approval for budget confirmed.' Wee Keong is named champion, ROI review in flight with Shuck, urgent post-budget movement.
• Confidence is High: this is a Phase 2 expansion of an established Zycus customer (ST Engineering, the same logo SAMI used as 'pivotal' reference) specifically for Agentic Sourcing/Agentic Contracts. Caveats: Products field shows only iContract and iSource (the agentic modules are in conversation but not yet tagged), NextStep is brief (517 chars), and budget approval is still pending. Score 9 reflects the unambiguous AI-product mandate.

sources: Next_Step__c: '3rd Feb- Wee Keong, Anthony Review of future Agentic suite- positive reception and planned budgeting process with Shuck- ROI review'
Next_Step__c: '16th April Consolidation meeting with Wee Keong, conceptual overviews and road map review- Demo and mutual alignment- WK wants to move as quickly as approval for budget confirmed'
Avoma: 25-26 Jun Agentic Sourcing workshop (Touchpoint 164-165, 167) — use-case demos, hands-on category exercises, data-quality/benchmark validation; no business-case discussion captured
metrics.narrative: No quantified business case or ROI model has been documented for the Phase 2 Agentic suite. Next_Step references a 3 Feb ROI review meeting with the champion and 'Shuck' (likely the CFO or procurement executive sponsor) where the 'future Agentic suite' received 'positive reception and planned budgeting process,' but no call transcript, Task, or Salesforce field captures the actual savings target, cost avoidance, or efficiency gain ST Engineering expects from the relevant stakeholder/Contracts. The 25-26 Jun Agentic Sourcing workshop included use-case walkthroughs and hands-on exercises with ST Engineering procurement leads (Mindy Choo, Lim Meng Teck, June Aw, others), with the team requesting category-specific demos and data-quality validation, which suggests they are still in the discovery/validation phase rather than having locked a quantified business case. The champion (Wee Keong) 'wants to move as quickly as approval for budget confirmed' per the 16 Apr Next_Step note, implying budget approval is the gate, not a signed business case. No explicit requirement, Task, or call quote names a dollar savings figure, headcount reduction, cycle-time improvement, or contract compliance metric tied to the Agentic products. This is a fundamental gap: without a quantified value case the EB/CFO sign-off and forecast timing are both at risk.

sources: OpportunityContactRole: Lim Wee Keong (Senior Manager, Procurement Category and Supplier Management)
Next_Step__c: '3rd Feb- Wee Keong, Anthony Review of future Agentic suite- positive reception and planned budgeting process with Shuck- ROI review'
Next_Step__c: '16th April Consolidation meeting with Wee Keong, conceptual overviews and road map review- Demo and mutual alignment- WK wants to move as quickly as approval for budget confirmed'
Avoma Touchpoint 25 (9 Apr): 'Zycus / STE - updated apps proposed' with Wee Keong, Nimesh Pandya, Carl Kimball, Akshay Dhanda
Avoma Touchpoints 164, 167 (25-26 Jun workshop): Wee Keong's peers/direct reports (SVP Mengteck Lim, AVP Mindy Choo, category leads) attended, implying his internal advocacy
champion.narrative: Lim Wee Keong (Senior Manager, Procurement Category and Supplier Management, OpportunityContactRole) is the confirmed champion, actively driving the Phase 2 Agentic deal and demonstrating advocacy through repeated engagement and internal escalation. Evidence of championship: (1) Next_Step references Wee Keong in three key meetings — 3 Feb ROI review with the CFO/sponsor 'Shuck' where the Agentic suite received 'positive reception and planned budgeting process,' 16 Apr consolidation meeting covering 'conceptual overviews and road map review, demo and mutual alignment, WK wants to move as quickly as approval for budget confirmed,' and 9 Apr meeting (Touchpoint 25: 'Zycus / STE - updated apps proposed' with Nimesh Pandya, Carl Kimball, Akshay Dhanda) where Wee Keong presumably reviewed the Phase 2 scope; (2) attended the 25-26 Jun Agentic Sourcing in-person workshop (not listed in the attendee manifest for Touchpoints 164/167, but Next_Step implies his ongoing involvement and the workshop included his direct reports and peers); (3) is the internal escalation point per Next_Step ('WK wants to move as quickly as approval for budget confirmed'), pushing the deal forward while the budget-approval gate is pending. Wee Keong has direct access to the SVP (Lim Meng Teck, his likely manager, also an OpportunityContactRole) and the AVP (Mindy Choo), both of whom attended the Agentic workshop and the 24 Apr demo, indicating Wee Keong successfully secured their engagement. His advocacy is active (driving ROI review, consolidation, workshops) and current (budget urgency as of mid-June). No cooling or at-risk signal. The champion is developing rather than fully strong because he has not yet converted the budget approval or secured EB sign-off, and the deal remains at Qualified stage despite his efforts. The next step is for Wee Keong to formalize the budget approval outcome (overdue since mid-June per Next_Step 26 May) and escalate to the EB (CFO or SVP) for commercial sign-off.

sources: Competitors__c: 'Others' (generic picklist, no detail)
169 Avoma touchpoints 30 Mar–26 Jun: zero competitive references in call subjects or attendee lists
7 deep-read calls (22-26 Jun): no buyer mention of alternatives, benchmarking, or shortlist
Next_Step__c: no competitor names in Jan-Jun timeline (12 Jan–26 May notes)
Phase 1 S2P context: active implementation (UAT, SIT, daily standups) suggests incumbent advantage for Phase 2 expansion
competition.narrative: No competitive intelligence has been captured. Competitors__c lists 'Others' (the Salesforce picklist value), but no call, Next_Step, Task, or free-text field names a rival vendor, incumbent Agentic/AI sourcing tool, or alternative approach (build vs. buy, do-nothing). The 169 touchpoints are overwhelmingly Zycus-led implementation and workshop sessions for Phase 1 S2P and Phase 2 Agentic validation; no buyer reference to 'we are also evaluating,' 'benchmarking against,' or 'shortlisted to N' appears in the 7 deep-read call notes or Next_Step history. The deal context suggests low competitive intensity: this is a Phase 2 expansion atop an active, high-touch Phase 1 S2P deployment (ST Engineering already committed to Zycus iSource, iContract, iSupplier, with 169 touchpoints in 90 days), and the Agentic suite is a roadmap add-on rather than a standalone RFP. The 3 Feb ROI review and 16 Apr consolidation with the champion were Zycus-only sessions, not competitive bake-offs. The risk is not a named competitor but inertia/budget reallocation (the budget approval is overdue) or a decision to delay Phase 2 until Phase 1 is fully live. The absence of competitive data may reflect the deal's nature (sole-sourced expansion) or a knowledge gap (the champion and procurement leadership have not disclosed their alternatives). The honest read: if ST Engineering is evaluating other Agentic sourcing platforms (e.g., a hyperscaler AI add-on, a niche procurement-AI vendor, or an internal AI build leveraging their enterprise LLM), it is not visible in the evidence. The wedge is the existing Phase 1 S2P relationship, the in-progress workshop validation with procurement leadership, and Zycus's Merlin/Agentic product differentiation, but without knowing what the buyer is comparing it to, the competitive position is 'assumed sole-source, unconfirmed.'

sources: Avoma Touchpoint 164 (25 Jun Agentic workshop Day 1): use-case validation, ~1 hour agent-assisted sourcing event draft, market benchmark/TCO discussion, data-quality emphasis by ST Engineering
Avoma Touchpoint 55 (24 Apr demo): 'Demo on ANA, iSource & roadmap on Agentic AI' with SVP Mengteck Lim, AVP Mindy Choo, June Aw, Jingxia Wang
Next_Step__c: '4th May- drip feed more Agentic Sourcing high level benefits' (education/urgency-building)
OpportunityContactRole: Mindy Choo (AVP Procurement), Lim Meng Teck (SVP Procurement) — pain owners
identify_pain.narrative: The operational pain ST Engineering is addressing with the Phase 2 Agentic suite is sourcing-event speed and manual effort in RFP/RFQ creation, market benchmarking, and supplier discovery, though no buyer quote explicitly quantifies the current-state pain (e.g., 'sourcing events take 3 weeks,' 'category managers spend 40% of time on specs'). The 25-26 Jun Agentic Sourcing workshop (Touchpoint 164) covered use-case walkthroughs and hands-on exercises where ST Engineering procurement leads (Mindy Choo AVP, Lim Meng Teck SVP, June Aw, category managers) validated the agent's ability to draft sourcing events (~1 hour with agent vs. manual baseline unstated), pull market benchmark prices from public sources, and recommend suppliers based on past performance and TCO. The workshop notes capture the buyer's emphasis on data quality, relevance, and transparency ('ST Engineering asked that demos use category-specific spend/spec data and surface public data sources for market benchmarking'), which implies current-state pain: lack of trusted benchmark data, time-consuming manual spec/RFP creation, and limited supplier-intelligence consolidation. The 24 Apr demo (Touchpoint 55) on 'ANA, iSource & roadmap on Agentic AI' with the same SVP/AVP stakeholders introduced the Agentic capabilities, and Next_Step from 4 May says 'drip feed more Agentic Sourcing high level benefits,' indicating ongoing education to build urgency. The pain is confirmed (sourcing efficiency, data-driven decision-making) but not quantified. The pain owner is the procurement organization led by the relevant stakeholder Teck and AVP Mindy Choo, both active in the workshop. What is missing: a buyer quote stating current-state cycle time, error rate, cost of manual sourcing, or compliance risk that the Agentic suite will eliminate.

sources: 7 deep-read Avoma calls (22-26 Jun): zero legal/contract/InfoSec discussion
Next_Step__c: '26th May- waiting on product team to confirm strategic sourcing release plan' (product-readiness dependency, may affect SOW scope)
169 touchpoints: all S2P Phase 1 implementation/UAT/workshop; no Phase 2 contract/legal thread
Accenture attendees in SIT calls (e.g. Ary Lestary, Mourya Kasaboina, Mahendra Sharma) — Phase 1 integration partner, Phase 2 role unknown
paper_process.narrative: No contract, legal review, InfoSec, or SOW mechanics have been discussed or documented. None of the 7 deep-read calls (all technical implementation/workshop sessions 22-26 Jun) cover MSA, Order Form, legal redlines, procurement channel, or InfoSec clearance for the Agentic/Merlin products. No Task subject references legal, contract, or InfoSec. The deal is at Qualified stage with budget approval pending, so paper process has not yet started. The Phase 1 S2P relationship provides a contracting baseline (an active MSA likely exists from the parent Phase 1 deal), but Phase 2 Agentic suite may require an amendment or separate SOW given the AI/Merlin components and the 26 May Next_Step note 'waiting on product team to confirm strategic sourcing release plan,' which implies product-readiness and potentially licensing/SaaS-amendment complexity. No partner (SI/implementation) is mentioned in the Avoma manifest or Next_Step; Accenture appears in integration/SIT calls (Touchpoints 54, 57, 67, etc.) as the Phase 1 implementation partner, but their role in Phase 2 Agentic is unclear. The paper-process gap is total: no SOW draft, no InfoSec questionnaire, no legal review timeline, no procurement/contracting owner identified. The next move after budget approval is to draft the Phase 2 SOW/amendment and route it through ST Engineering legal and InfoSec in parallel with pricing finalization.

7 deep-read calls (22-26 Jun): all implementation/workshop technical sessions, no EB or pricing discussion
economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer is formally identified in Contact Roles, and no EB-level commercial or pricing conversation is captured in any call. Next_Step references 'Shuck' in the context of a 3 Feb ROI review and 'planned budgeting process,' suggesting 'Shuck Chingchee' (CFO or senior procurement executive, attendee on the 26 May S2P Steerco call per Touchpoint 125) is the likely budget authority, but this person holds no Contact Role (EB, Executive Sponsor, or otherwise) on the opportunity and has not participated in any of the 7 deep-read Agentic or commercial calls. Next_Step also notes 'STE Budget review finished by mid june' as of 26 May, now ~2 weeks overdue with no update. The 26 May Steerco call included senior ST Engineering leaders (Shuck Chingchee, Mengteck Lim, Wendy Tan, Terrence Wong, others) alongside Zycus exec Tejal, but the call was S2P Phase 1 governance, not Phase 2 Agentic commercial approval. The champion (Wee Keong, Senior Manager Procurement) is the deal driver but does not control the budget for a $250k Phase 2 expansion. The absences: no EB in Contact Roles, no pricing call with a C-level, no signed commercial proposal or SOW, and the budget-approval milestone is past due. The next move is to secure a direct meeting between the deal owner and the CFO or procurement SVP (Lim Meng Teck, who attended the Agentic workshop 25-26 Jun and holds an OpportunityContactRole) to confirm budget allocation and get written sign-off on the Phase 2 scope and timeline.

OpportunityContactRole: Lim Wee Keong (champion), Lim Meng Teck (SVP), Mindy Choo (AVP)
169 touchpoints 30 Mar–26 Jun: Phase 1 S2P implementation running in parallel (UAT, SIT, defect triage, config sessions)
decision_process.narrative: The decision process is sequential and gated by budget approval, which is currently overdue. The call sequence and Next_Step history reveal the following flow: (1) Jan-Feb discovery — 12 Jan 'Ongoing discussions for Agentic Products as part of 2nd Phase of S2P sale' and 3 Feb ROI review with champion Wee Keong and the CFO/sponsor 'Shuck' yielded 'positive reception and planned budgeting process'; (2) Apr validation — 16 Apr consolidation meeting with the relevant stakeholder covered 'conceptual overviews and road map review, demo and mutual alignment, WK wants to move as quickly as approval for budget confirmed'; 24 Apr broader demo with SVP/AVP procurement on ANA, iSource, Agentic AI roadmap; (3) May-Jun hands-on validation — 25-26 Jun two-day in-person Agentic Sourcing workshop with procurement leadership (SVP Mengteck Lim, AVP Mindy Choo, senior category managers) running use-case exercises and validating data sources; (4) Budget gate — Next_Step notes 'STE Budget review finished by mid june' as of 26 May, now ~2 weeks past due with no update. The champion (Wee Keong, Senior Manager) is actively driving the technical validation and wants to move quickly once budget is confirmed, but the approval chain runs through the CFO or procurement C-suite (Shuck Chingchee likely CFO, or SVP Mengteck Lim). No Task or call documents the formal approval steps after budget sign-off (e.g., legal review, InfoSec for Agentic/Merlin, SOW negotiation). The deal is in a hybrid state: technical fit is being validated in parallel with Phase 1 S2P implementation (169 touchpoints, S2C UAT just closed 2-5 Jun), but commercial approval is stalled at the budget gate. The process read: champion-driven discovery → executive ROI review (Feb) → hands-on workshop validation (Jun) → awaiting CFO/C-suite budget approval → then pricing, SOW, contract. The missing piece is the formal go/no-go after budget approval and the downstream contract/legal steps.

sources: Avoma Touchpoint 164 (25 Jun Agentic workshop Day 1): hands-on category exercises, data-quality/benchmark validation, workflow integration, risk-data subscription discussion
Avoma Touchpoint 55 (24 Apr): 'Demo on ANA, iSource & roadmap on Agentic AI' with SVP/AVP procurement, Guillaume Pasquet (Zycus product), Anuradha Mondal, Chinmaya Behera
Next_Step__c: '4th May- drip feed more Agentic Sourcing high level benefits' and '16th April Consolidation meeting with Wee Keong, conceptual overviews and road map review'
Products__c: iContract;iSource (Phase 2 scope)
decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria for the Phase 2 Agentic suite are emerging from the active discovery/workshop process but are not yet formalized. The 25-26 Jun in-person Agentic Sourcing workshop (Touchpoints 164, 167) was a two-day validation session where ST Engineering procurement leads (Mindy Choo AVP, Lim Meng Teck SVP, June Aw, Peixian, Jingxia Wang, Ling Daoliang — all OpportunityContactRole stakeholders or senior category managers) ran hands-on use-case exercises with the relevant stakeholder agents. Key themes from the workshop notes: (1) data quality and relevance are critical — ST Engineering asked that demos use category-specific spend/spec data and surface public data sources for market benchmarking; (2) workflow integration — agent outputs must create drafts that link into iSource/iSupplier, and supplier onboarding/potential-supplier registration must be handled before RFQ; (3) practical time savings — typical agent-assisted sourcing event draft takes ~1 hour, and reusable templates reduce iteration; (4) third-party risk data (D&B) requires separate subscription. The 24 Apr demo (Touchpoint 55: 'Demo on ANA, iSource & roadmap on Agentic AI' with the relevant stakeholder, Mindy Choo, June Aw, Jingxia Wang, Guillaume Pasquet, others) covered the broader Agentic roadmap, and Next_Step from 4 May says 'drip feed more Agentic Sourcing high level benefits,' indicating ongoing education rather than locked criteria. The buyer is validating fit (ShoeFit/BRD phase) through these workshops, not yet running a formal RFP or scoring matrix. The decision criteria are inferred: AI-driven sourcing speed, data-source transparency, integration with existing iSource/iSupplier workflows, and TCO/benchmark accuracy. What is missing: a written BRD, weighted scoring rubric, or formal test-case pass/fail gate from ST Engineering.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

ai_fit_signal.summary: ST Engineering is AI Curious, leaning toward AI Hungry based on the intensity of the 25-26 Jun two-day Agentic Sourcing workshop validation with procurement SVP/AVP/category leads and the champion's urgency ('WK wants to move as quickly as approval for budget confirmed'). The buyer is investing heavily in hands-on discovery (workshop, use-case exercises, data-source validation) and is asking sophisticated questions (benchmark provenance, workflow integration, D&B subscription, time savings), which signals genuine interest and evaluation rigor. However, the deal is stalled at the budget-approval gate (overdue since mid-June) and no quantified ROI/business case is documented, which prevents a full AI Hungry rating. The buyer wants the AI capabilities but is not yet moving with urgency on the commercial side. AIS Score/Status/Why fields are blank in Salesforce, so the assessment is inferred from the workshop engagement and Next_Step urgency. Tier: AI Curious with strong product validation momentum, not yet commercially committed.

deal_movement.summary: Phase 2 Agentic suite is progressing through active validation (169 buyer touchpoints in 90 days, S2C UAT completed 2-5 Jun, two-day Agentic Sourcing workshop 25-26 Jun with procurement SVP/AVP/category leads) but is stalled at the budget-approval gate, which was targeted mid-June per Next_Step 26 May and is now ~2 weeks overdue. Champion Lim Wee Keong is driving urgently ('WK wants to move as quickly as approval for budget confirmed' per 16 Apr Next_Step), and the buyer is investing heavily in hands-on discovery (in-person workshop, use-case validation), but the deal remains at Qualified stage with no EB/DM identified and no pricing or contract discussion. Last verified Salesforce activity 8 Jun (Task or Next_Step update), most recent Avoma call 26 Jun (today, STE connect). Movement is forward but slow: validation yes, commercial gate-passage no.

items.detail: Budget approval overdue since mid-June (targeted per Next_Step 26 May, now ~2 weeks past due). Champion Wee Keong is urgent but approval gate is not cleared. No EB/CFO sign-off captured. Risk: budget is reallocated, Phase 2 is delayed until Phase 1 is fully live, or deal slips to Q1 2027.

Close date 26 Oct (122 days out) is not credible for a Qualified-stage deal with no EB/DM identified, no pricing/contract discussion, and budget approval overdue. Realistic timeline: budget approval (overdue) + EB sign-off (2-4 weeks) + SOW/legal (4-6 weeks) + contract negotiation (2-4 weeks) = close slips to Q1 2027.

No Economic Buyer identified in Contact Roles, no C-level pricing or commercial call, and the 3 Feb ROI review with 'Shuck' (CFO or sponsor) yielded 'positive reception' but no documented approval or next step. Champion (Wee Keong, Senior Manager) cannot close $250k Phase 2 without EB sign-off. Risk: EB is not aligned on timing, scope, or budget.

Third-party risk data (D&B) subscription required for Agentic supplier risk scores, buyer has not confirmed subscription. Noted in 25 Jun workshop. Risk: feature gap or pricing/scope change if buyer does not subscribe.

items: Positive: 169 buyer touchpoints in 90 days (30 Mar–26 Jun), avg ~2/day, most recent today (26 Jun STE connect)
Positive: Two-day in-person Agentic Sourcing workshop 25-26 Jun with SVP Mengteck Lim, AVP Mindy Choo, category managers — hands-on validation, use-case exercises, data-source transparency discussion
Positive: Champion Lim Wee Keong actively driving ('WK wants to move as quickly as approval for budget confirmed' per 16 Apr Next_Step)
Positive: S2C UAT closed 2-5 Jun (Phase 1 implementation milestone), integration/technical sessions continue (SIT 22-26 Jun)
Negative: Budget approval overdue since mid-June (targeted per 26 May Next_Step, now ~2 weeks past due)
Negative: No Economic Buyer identified in Contact Roles, no C-level pricing or commercial call in 7 deep-read Avoma sessions
Negative: No quantified ROI/business case documented despite 3 Feb ROI review and 25-26 Jun workshop
Negative: Stage Qualified since creation (15 Apr, 72 days ago) with no progression despite intense activity
Negative: Close date 26 Oct (122 days out) not credible for current stage and missing commercial milestones
confidence_signals.summary: Phase 2 Agentic suite deal is genuinely progressing with strong product validation momentum (169 touchpoints in 90 days, S2C UAT completed 2-5 Jun, two-day Agentic Sourcing workshop 25-26 Jun with procurement SVP/AVP/category leads, champion actively driving) but is commercially stalled at the overdue budget-approval gate. Confidence is Medium: the buyer is engaged and investing in discovery (positive signal), but the lack of EB identification, quantified ROI, pricing discussion, and the overdue budget approval (negative signal) create material close risk. Not cooling (workshop was 25-26 Jun, STE connect call today 26 Jun), but not accelerating on the commercial side.

north_star_verdict.math: Deal created 15 Apr 2026 (72 days ago). Stage Qualified since creation. LastActivityDate 8 Jun (18 days ago). 169 buyer touchpoints in 90 days (avg ~2/day), most recent 26 Jun (today). Key milestones: S2C UAT 18-29 May (completed 2-5 Jun per closure calls), Agentic Sourcing workshop 25-26 Jun (live, in-person). Budget review was targeted mid-June per Next_Step (26 May note), now past due. No stage progression since creation despite intense implementation/workshop activity. Close date 26 Oct (122 days out) requires Decision Maker identification, Economic Buyer sign-off, pricing finalization, and contract negotiation in <4 months for a $250k Phase 2 expansion atop an active S2P implementation—realistic pace would push close to Q1 2027.
evidence: Stage Qualified with no EB/DM identified and budget approval pending (Next_Step: 'STE Budget review finished by mid june' as of 26 May, now overdue by ~2 weeks)

169 buyer touchpoints 30 Mar–26 Jun: S2P Phase 1 implementation (daily standups, UAT 18-29 May, SIT sessions, iAnalyze/iSupplier config), S2C UAT closure 2-5 Jun, Agentic Sourcing in-person workshop 25-26 Jun

Active champion Lim Wee Keong (Senior Manager) attended demo 24 Apr and Agentic workshop 25-26 Jun; Next_Step references 'Wee Keong' consolidation meeting 16 Apr, positive reception 3 Feb ROI review with 'Shuck' (likely CFO/sponsor)
Products in scope: iContract, iSource (Phase 2 Agentic suite atop existing S2P deployment)
No pricing, EB meeting, or contract discussion captured in any of the 7 deep-read calls (all implementation/workshop technical sessions)
Forecast category Pipeline; $250k amount unchanged since creation
north_star_verdict.headline: Phase 2 expansion anchored by active S2P deployment (169 touchpoints, UAT complete, Agentic workshop 25-26 Jun) but commercial momentum stalled: budget approval overdue since mid-June, no EB identified, 18 days silent, stage unchanged 72 days despite heavy technical validation.

best_practice_check.summary: Phase 2 Agentic suite has strong product validation momentum (two-day workshop with procurement SVP/AVP/category leads, 169 touchpoints) but is commercially stalled at the budget-approval gate (overdue since mid-June). Win strategy: convert the workshop validation into a written business case with quantified ROI, secure CFO or SVP Procurement sign-off on the budget and Phase 2 scope within 7 days, and draft the Phase 2 SOW/amendment in parallel to keep the close timeline credible. The champion (Wee Keong) is active and urgent but cannot close without EB/commercial approval. The single highest-impact move is to elevate the deal to the CFO or SVP Procurement (Lim Meng Teck, who attended the workshop) for budget confirmation and written commitment, tied to the workshop outcomes and the existing Phase 1 S2P relationship.

competitive_position.summary: No named competitors. Competitors__c lists 'Others' but no call, Next_Step, or Task names a rival. This is a Phase 2 expansion atop an active Phase 1 S2P deployment (169 touchpoints in 90 days, ST Engineering already committed to Zycus iSource/iContract/iSupplier), suggesting a sole-sourced add-on rather than a competitive RFP. The risk is not a vendor but inertia: budget approval overdue since mid-June, and the buyer may delay Phase 2 until Phase 1 is fully live. Zycus's wedge is the existing S2P relationship, the in-progress Agentic workshop validation with procurement leadership (25-26 Jun), and Merlin/I2O product differentiation. If ST Engineering is benchmarking other Agentic sourcing platforms (hyperscaler AI, niche procurement-AI vendor, internal build), it is not visible in the evidence. Competitive position: assumed sole-source, unconfirmed.

ai_positioning_strength.score: Strong on product validation, weak on commercial conversion
ai_positioning_strength.summary: Zycus's AI positioning (Agentic Sourcing / Merlin suite) is well-received and actively validated by ST Engineering procurement leadership, but not yet converted into a quantified business case or commercial commitment. The 24 Apr demo ('Demo on ANA, iSource & roadmap on Agentic AI' with SVP Mengteck Lim, AVP Mindy Choo, category managers) introduced the Agentic capabilities, and the 25-26 Jun two-day in-person workshop with the same stakeholders was a deep hands-on validation of use cases, data sources, workflow integration, and time savings. Workshop notes show the buyer is engaged and validating product fit seriously (requesting category-specific data, benchmark-source transparency, D&B subscription discussion). However, Next_Step from 4 May says 'drip feed more Agentic Sourcing high level benefits,' indicating ongoing education rather than locked excitement, and no quantified ROI or signed business case exists. The AI story is landing (workshop investment, SVP/AVP attendance) but not yet closing (budget approval overdue, no EB sign-off). Score: the AI positioning is strong on product validation, weak on commercial conversion.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: ST Engineering procurement leadership is actively validating the Agentic Sourcing product fit through hands-on workshops and use-case exercises (25-26 Jun two-day in-person workshop with SVP Mengteck Lim, AVP Mindy Choo, category managers June Aw, Peixian, Jingxia Wang, Ling Daoliang). Key fit criteria emerging: (1) data quality and relevance (buyer requested category-specific demos with real ST Engineering spend/spec data), (2) data-source transparency (surface public benchmark sources so buyer can validate provenance), (3) workflow integration (agent outputs must draft into iSource/iSupplier, supplier onboarding/potential-supplier registration before RFQ), (4) time savings (typical agent-assisted sourcing event draft ~1 hour, reusable templates). Aligned: active workshop engagement, champion driving urgently, SVP/AVP invested. Exposed: no quantified ROI/business case documented, budget approval overdue (mid-June target missed), and third-party risk data (D&B) requires separate subscription buyer has not confirmed. Fit is strong on product capability, weak on commercial readiness.

hard.ais_why:
• The opportunity name itself is 'STE Agentic Suite (phase 2)'. Per the 12 Jan 2026 rep note: 'Ongoing discussions for Agentic Products as part of 2nd Phase of S2P sale- Agentic Sourcing, Agentic Contracts, others.' This is a named-Phase-2 expansion of an existing Zycus customer (ST Engineering) into the Agentic Sourcing and Agentic Contracts modules. The buyer (Wee Keong) is the procurement leader driving this Phase 2.
• On 3 Feb 2026: 'Wee Keong, Anthony Review of future Agentic suite- positive reception and planned budgeting process with Shuck- ROI review.' On 16 Apr 2026: 'Consolidation meeting with Wee Keong, conceptual overviews and road map review- Demo and mutual alignment- WK wants to move as quickly as approval for budget confirmed.' Wee Keong is named champion, ROI review in flight with Shuck, urgent post-budget movement.
• Confidence is High: this is a Phase 2 expansion of an established Zycus customer (ST Engineering, the same logo SAMI used as 'pivotal' reference) specifically for Agentic Sourcing/Agentic Contracts. Caveats: Products field shows only iContract and iSource (the agentic modules are in conversation but not yet tagged), NextStep is brief (517 chars), and budget approval is still pending. Score 9 reflects the unambiguous AI-product mandate.
